key_insight: ATLAS learns a diffusion-based sampler that directly generates Boltzmann-distributed amorphous structures from an energy function, enabling efficient sampling, thermodynamic estimation, and property steering in rugged glassy landscapes.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to model and design metallic glasses and other amorphous materials with far fewer energy evaluations than conventional molecular simulation.
method: Equivariant graph neural network parameterizes a time-reversed diffusion sampler.
Works across system size, temperature, and composition, including expensive ML interatomic potentials.
Uses sampling-time guidance to estimate free energies/entropies and steer toward target observables.
evidence_signal: In 2D Kob-Andersen systems, ATLAS matches parallel-tempering MCMC distributions and achieves below 0.2% free-energy error in the low-temperature glass regime with over 500-fold fewer energy evaluations; it also recovers experimental short-range-order trends in Cu-Zr and Cr-Co-Ni glasses.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the target energy model; composition-amortized pretraining and LLM-guided search may still be limited by oracle accuracy and extrapolation outside the training distribution.
paper_title: ATLAS: A Foundation Neural Sampler for Amorphous Materials
